content: Backup verification MVP pattern: (1) non-zero file size check: [ -s "$DUMP_FILE" ] — exits false if file missing or empty; (2) structural integrity check: docker compose exec -T database pg_restore --list "$DUMP_FILE_IN_CONTAINER" > /dev/null 2>&1 — exits non-zero if archive is corrupt or unreadable. Note: pg_restore --list cannot fully detect all corruption; it reads the TOC (table of contents) only. A zero-size file passes pg_dump exit code because pg_dump may exit 0 with no data if -T flag issue causes premature pipe close. Both checks together catch the most common failure modes. pg_verifybackup exists but only for pg_basebackup physical backups, not pg_dump logical backups.
